preamble: Nasalization of a liquid occurs when a lateral [l] shifts to a nasal [n]. This can be triggered by surrounding nasal sounds or as a sporadic dialectal variation in liquid-nasal alternation.

examples.note: Occasional shift of lateral to nasal in certain rural dialects or in specific phonological environments.
Liquids often alternate with nasals in several East Asian language families, particularly in word-initial positions.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
